Root cause: lisa avatar doesn't support 'graceful' style.
Verified: API returns 201, job completes with video URL in ~10s.
Also added SPEECH_SERVICE_ENDPOINT to local .env.

test: add all-invalid-timestamps tests for parse_vscode_log (#543)

Add two tests covering the untested code path where all lines match
CCREQ_RE but every timestamp is unparseable:

- TestParseVscodeLog::test_all_lines_invalid_timestamp_returns_empty_list
  Verifies parse_vscode_log returns [] (not None) when all regex-matching
  lines have invalid timestamps.

- TestGetVscodeSummary::test_all_invalid_timestamps_still_counted_in_log_files_parsed
  Verifies such files are still counted in log_files_parsed (since the
  return is not None).

additional regression safety.

instead of filtering then re-searching. Each test now iterates

fix: Web Speech API continuous mode + auto-restart on Edge

- continuous=true so recognition doesn't stop after first result
- Auto-restart on onend when still in listening state (Edge quirk)
- Ignore no-speech errors (silence is normal)
- stateRef to avoid stale closure in onend callback
- Voice text inserted into InputForm, user confirms before sending

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf: skip redundant sort in _render_session_table (#541)

_render_session_table unconditionally re-sorted its input on every call,
but the input is always pre-sorted by get_all_sessions() and order is
preserved by _filter_sessions() and downstream list comprehensions.

Replace the unconditional sorted() with a pre_sorted parameter
(default True) so the common render path avoids the O(n log n) cost.
Callers with unsorted input can pass pre_sorted=False to opt in.

Add TestRenderSessionTablePreSorted with two tests:
- Verifies pre-sorted input renders in correct descending order
- Verifies pre_sorted=False explicitly sorts unsorted input

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Add user_impersonation permission on Cognitive Services API
- Admin consent granted
- MSAL: popup → redirect mode (Edge COOP blocks popups)
- Scope: .default → user_impersonation (explicit delegation)
- Handle InteractionRequiredAuthError with redirect fallback

Replace V-classifier with V-flow matching; mask U-flow dynamics not just loss (#2)

* Initial plan

* Add run_modal_easy.py and results for full pipeline at null_prob=0.95

Co-authored-by: jacksonloper <2056977+jacksonloper@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: jacksonloper <2056977+jacksonloper@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: jacksonloper <2056977+jacksonloper@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address code review: improve comments in run_modal_easy.py and run_modal_diag.py

Co-authored-by: jacksonloper <2056977+jacksonloper@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add run_modal_experiment.py framework and first case study results (case_np95_pihat95)

Co-authored-by: jacksonloper <2056977+jacksonloper@users.noreply.github.com>

* Introduce softplus(rho) shape nonlinearity and rho=-10 null marks

The shape function now uses softplus(rho) * exp(...) instead of rho * exp(...).
Null marks use rho=-10 (softplus(-10) ≈ 4.5e-5) instead of rho=0, giving
real background-like noise and allowing the model to represent 'zero' with
a smooth gradient signal.

Changes applied consistently across:
- model.py (shape_fn, compute_X, compute_X_batched, sampling)
- run_modal_experiment.py (inline copies, diagnostics, plots)
- train.py (diagnostics, NaN replacement)
- run_modal.py, run_modal_easy.py, run_modal_oracle.py, run_modal_diag.py

Co-authored-by: jacksonloper <2056977+jacksonloper@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add softplus experiment results: Phase II stable at frac=0.952 (target 0.95)

The softplus(rho) nonlinearity completely resolves the Phase II bootstrap
instability. Post-Phase-I frac=0.956 stays stable throughout 150k Phase II
steps, finishing at 0.952. Compare with old linear model where frac degraded
from 0.858 to 0.358.

Co-authored-by: jacksonloper <2056977+jacksonloper@users.noreply.github.com>

* Clean up: remove pre-softplus results, obsolete scripts, and old design doc

Deleted:
- results/case_np95_pihat95/ (pre-softplus experiment)
- All root-level results (diag_*, easy_*, oracle_*, main pipeline outputs)
- run_modal.py, run_modal_diag.py, run_modal_easy.py, run_modal_oracle.py
  (all superseded by the parameterized run_modal_experiment.py)
- DETAILED_PLAN.md (pre-softplus design notes)

Updated README.md to document:
- Softplus shape function and rho=-10 null marks
- Streamlined file listing (4 Python files + results/)
- How to run experiments and configure cases
- Current results (case_softplus_np95_pihat95)

Co-authored-by: jacksonloper <2056977+jacksonloper@users.noreply.github.com>

- Single-page app explaining the Gaussian bump setup, approach, and findings

* Add GT eval loss tracking and loss_curves.png to run_modal_experiment.py

- Add eval_flow_loss JIT function for gradient-free loss evaluation
- Generate fixed eval batch of 512 ground-truth (X, U) pairs
- Phase I: track both training loss (pihat) and GT eval loss every 200 steps
- Phase II: track GT eval loss every 2500 steps
- Generate loss_curves.png with two panels (Phase I + Phase II)

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/prob-ml/admixture-identifiability/sessions/dcd92147-d90f-4c13-a8fa-4c794dda6704

Co-authored-by: jacksonloper <2056977+jacksonloper@users.noreply.github.com>

* Rerun experiment with GT eval loss tracking, update website with loss curves

- New loss_curves.png: Phase I (training + GT eval) and Phase II (GT eval only)
- Updated all result images and training log from fresh run
- Website App.tsx: add Training Loss Curves section with explanation
- Updated key metrics to match new run (0.954/0.954 vs 0.950 true)

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/prob-ml/admixture-identifiability/sessions/dcd92147-d90f-4c13-a8fa-4c794dda6704

Co-authored-by: jacksonloper <2056977+jacksonloper@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add oracle model comparison in Phase II loss curves

In Phase II, train a second "oracle" model from scratch on fresh ground-truth
(X,U) pairs alongside the bootstrap model. Both are evaluated on the same
fixed GT eval batch. The Phase II loss plot shows both losses with smart
y-limits: [min(oracle_min, bootstrap_min), min(oracle_max, bootstrap_max)]
to avoid showing very large early oracle losses.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/prob-ml/admixture-identifiability/sessions/1ed69a1f-f07f-4384-8c2e-08a3e0b95e0f

Co-authored-by: jacksonloper <2056977+jacksonloper@users.noreply.github.com>

* Rerun experiment with oracle comparison, update results and website

New loss_curves.png shows bootstrap vs oracle GT eval losses in Phase II.
Oracle model starts from scratch and trains on true (X,U) pairs as a
reference baseline. Updated App.tsx with explanation of the oracle
comparison and updated metrics (0.955/0.949).

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/prob-ml/admixture-identifiability/sessions/1ed69a1f-f07f-4384-8c2e-08a3e0b95e0f

Co-authored-by: jacksonloper <2056977+jacksonloper@users.noreply.github.com>

* Rewrite run_modal_experiment.py to binary-V two-model architecture

Key changes:
- Shape function: V * rho * exp(...) instead of softplus(rho) * exp(...)
- Null marks: V=0, rho=0, logsigma=0 (instead of rho=-10)
- All sampling returns (X, V, U) tuples with binary V
- Two networks: VClassifierMLP (X → V logits) + VelocityMLP (conditioned on V)
- Masked flow matching loss: only V=1 positions contribute
- V-classifier trained with binary cross-entropy
- Phase I trains both V-classifier and U-flow on pihat data
- Phase II: bootstrap (V-classifier + U-flow from Phase I) vs oracle (fresh)
- Diagnostics: frac(V=0) replaces frac(softplus(rho)<0.1), V-acc replaces mean|rho|
- Scatter plots separate V=0/V=1 marks, use rho directly
- Survival/marginal plots use rho for V=1 marks only
- Loss curves show V-classifier loss in Phase I, masked U-flow in Phase II
- case_name updated to case_v_binary_np95_pihat95

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: jacksonloper <2056977+jacksonloper@users.noreply.github.com>

* Refactor to binary-V two-model architecture

Replace softplus(rho) shape with V·rho gating. Add binary latent V
per mark: V=0 means null (rho=logsigma=0), V=1 means active.

Two models trained simultaneously:
1. VClassifierMLP: predicts V|X (binary cross-entropy)
2. VelocityMLP: flow matching for U conditioned on V, with masked loss
   (only V=1 positions contribute)

Updated model.py, flow_matching.py, train.py, and run_modal_experiment.py.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/prob-ml/admixture-identifiability/sessions/4a5da184-8894-4091-b779-0e6e4b948cf0

Co-authored-by: jacksonloper <2056977+jacksonloper@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update README and website for binary-V two-model architecture

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/prob-ml/admixture-identifiability/sessions/4a5da184-8894-4091-b779-0e6e4b948cf0

Co-authored-by: jacksonloper <2056977+jacksonloper@users.noreply.github.com>

* Replace V-classifier with V-flow and mask U-flow dynamics

1. Replace VClassifierMLP (independent binary logits, BCE loss) with
   VFlowMLP (rectified flow matching for joint V distribution, rounded
   to {0,1} at inference).

2. Mask interpolated state z_s, target velocity, and predicted velocity
   at V=0 positions in U-flow (not just the loss). Also mask initial
   noise and velocity at each Euler step during ODE sampling.

Updated: run_modal_experiment.py, flow_matching.py, train.py, website,
and README.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/prob-ml/admixture-identifiability/sessions/7ea80692-e89c-4136-80c8-afea18e043aa

commit.message:
feat: fix agent workflow — corrected plan to Agent4, destination-aware images, video polling

Workflow fixes:
- Agent4 now receives Agent3's regulation-checked plan (not original)
- Destination extracted from plan for accurate image generation
- Agent4 instructions: destination-aware prompts, no cross-region landmarks
- Right panel Plan tab shows only corrected plan (not full regulation output)

Photo Avatar:
- poll_video_job(): polls batch synthesis API until Succeeded/Failed
- Video URL delivered as SSE TEXT event with content_type='video'
- Mock mode includes video event for demo

Azure cleanup:
- Deleted orphaned func-5gg4m4g72lrdo + App Service Plan
- Added SEARCH_ENDPOINT, SEARCH_API_KEY, CONTENT_UNDERSTANDING_ENDPOINT,
  COSMOS_DB_ENDPOINT to .env

now produces identical output.

Merge Aurora publishing features into the CLI for a unified read+write tool:

- Add publish, create, and diff commands (Markdown → Confluence)
- Add storage format output (-o storage) for lossless round-trip editing

- Add auth fallback: Keychain → CONFLUENCE_PAT env var
- Add Jira macro handler, table/strikethrough plugins, flattenTableCells
- Add image upload, layout preservation, frontmatter-based workflow
- Update page command to output YAML frontmatter for round-trip publish

feat: add model selector UI + remove unnecessary input truncation

- Pass selected model to all 4 agents via deployment_name override

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Normalize HTTP proxy request framing and connection handling (#84)

* fix: relax HTTP tunnel first-byte timeout

* test: cover tunnel first-byte timeout regression

* Fix replicated target round-robin

* Buffer HTTP requests before proxy routing

* Fall back to direct proxy when pipeline setup fails

* Harden HTTP request parsing and forwarding

* Add HTTP proxy integration suite

* Define single-request HTTP proxy contract

* Update mesh-llm/src/tunnel.rs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix chunked proxy request buffering

* Replace hand-rolled HTTP header parsing with httparse

httparse is already a transitive dependency (via reqwest → hyper) so this
adds zero new binary weight. It provides RFC-correct, fuzz-tested,
SIMD-accelerated header parsing instead of manual split_once/find/lines
iteration.

Changes:
- Add httparse as a direct dependency
- Replace read_until_header_end + find_header_end + content_length +
  header_value + header_has_token with read_until_headers_parsed() that
  uses httparse::Request::parse()
- Replace hand-rolled header iteration in finalize_forwarded_request()
  with httparse re-parse
- Handle RFC 7230 §3.3.3: when both Transfer-Encoding and Content-Length
  are present, Transfer-Encoding wins
- Chunked body decoder and all downstream routing code unchanged

* Add streaming proxy integration tests

* Clean up dead code from httparse migration

Remove extract_model_from_http, extract_session_hint, extract_body_json,
and 9 tests that exercised them — these were the old peek-style helpers
that are no longer called by production code after the httparse switch.

Also remove unused affinity::self import.

* Fix streaming responses: don't half-close TCP to upstream

llama-server interprets a TCP FIN (from shutdown(SHUT_WR)) as a client
disconnect and stops sending SSE chunks. The Connection: close header
already signals a one-shot connection, so the half-close is redundant
and actively breaks streaming.

This was a pre-existing bug in the buffer-and-forward approach — main's
peek-and-relay never half-closed because it used bidirectional copy.

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Michael Neale <michael.neale@gmail.com>

commit.message:
fix: add 500/server_error to retryable errors, truncate Agent3 input to 4000 chars

Server 500 errors from Foundry are transient and should be retried.
Long plan markdown (5000+ chars) can cause context overflow when
combined with Web Search + KB tool results. Truncate to 4000 chars.

fix(vscode_parser): incremental aggregation in get_vscode_summary (#523)

Refactor get_vscode_summary to process each log file's requests
incrementally via a mutable _SummaryAccumulator, discarding each
file's request list before moving to the next. This drops peak
memory from O(total_requests) to O(max_requests_in_one_file).

- Extract _update_vscode_summary helper for in-place accumulation
- Extract _finalize_summary to convert accumulator to frozen summary
- Rewrite build_vscode_summary to delegate to the new helpers
- Add test_incremental_aggregation verifying per-file processing
